summary: Russia intensified missile and drone attacks across multiple fronts, notably a ballistic strike on Priluk and large‑scale Shahed raids on Kharkiv, Odesa and the north. Ukrainian air defenses claimed 180 enemy UAVs downed, while Russian Rubicon drones penetrated deep behind Ukrainian lines. Both sides report heightened aerial activity and new weapon deployments, including a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal launch from Savasiysk.
key_points: Ballistic missile hit central Priluk, killing 2, wounding 17, damaging a factory, mall and supermarket (UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian forces shot down 180 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones, with 27 strikes recorded across north, south and east (kpszsu/62230).
Russian Rubicon unit deployed dual‑camera drones deep behind Ukrainian lines, increasing interceptions of Ukrainian UAVs and night bombers (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
MiG‑31K launched from Savasiysk airfield carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (mon1tor_ua/66835).
Large Shahed swarm (≈20) entered Odesa from the Black Sea, striking residential areas (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Ukrainian troops trapped north of Pokrovsk in a Russian‑controlled kill zone, with pilots dominating approaches (ukr_leaks_eng/30282).

Multiple drone groups penetrated Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia regions, targeting Chuhuiv, Pavlograd, Kamyanske and other locales (kpszsu/62214, kpszsu/62204, kpszsu/62260, kpszsu/62202, kpszsu/62216).
Kharkiv night attack damaged 25 houses and a high‑rise, injuring three civilians; Odesa’s Izmail district warehouse hit, causing fire (UkraineNow/81303).

sections.executive_summary: A Russian ballistic missile strike on Priluk killed two civilians and injured 17, marking the deadliest single attack reported today. Ukrainian air defenses reported downing 180 hostile drones, yet Russian Rubicon UAVs penetrated deep behind lines, and a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal was launched from Savasiysk. Simultaneous drone swarms assaulted Kharkiv, Odesa and the north, indicating a broad escalation across multiple fronts.

points: Ukrainian air defense units across the country intercepted 180 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas models, preventing further damage (kpszsu/62230).
The 7th Airborne Assault Corps reported its forces north of Pokrovsk are encircled in a Russian‑controlled kill zone, with enemy pilots dominating all approaches (ukr_leaks_eng/30282).
In Kharkiv, three civilians were injured and 25 private houses plus a high‑rise building were damaged by two Russian strikes, highlighting ongoing urban combat (UkraineNow/81303).
Ukrainian reconnaissance noted increased interceptions of Russian Rubicon drones equipped with dual day/thermal cameras, which are now striking deep behind front lines (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian forces reported successful destruction of 27 enemy UAV strike points across 15 locations, limiting material losses (kpszsu/62230).
Ukrainian territorial defense units engaged multiple drone groups approaching from Belgorod, Sumy and Kharkiv borders, maintaining airspace integrity (kpszsu/62202).
Ukrainian command confirmed no use of Latvian territory or airspace for drone operations, countering Russian intelligence claims (ukr_leaks_eng/30284).
Russia launched a ballistic missile at Priluk, destroying a commercial complex and causing two civilian deaths and 17 injuries (UkraineNow/81317).
The Rubicon UAV unit deployed advanced drones with dual cameras, striking targets far behind Ukrainian lines and engaging Ukrainian UAVs (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
A MiG‑31K fighter took off from Savasiysk airfield carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, indicating escalation of long‑range strike capability (mon1tor_ua/66835).
Russian forces coordinated multiple Shahed drone waves from the Black Sea into Odesa, delivering at least 20 UAVs in a single sortie (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Russian pilots established air superiority north of Pokrovsk, creating a kill zone that threatens Ukrainian units attempting to withdraw (ukr_leaks_eng/30282).
Russian drone groups from Belgorod entered Ukrainian airspace along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, targeting logistical nodes (kpszsu/62202).
Russian statements claim Ukrainian anti‑missile systems are failing, while denying any diplomatic progress, reinforcing a hardline stance (tass_agency/376588).
Ukrainian troops near Pokrovsk (Krasnoarmeysk) are trapped in a Russian‑controlled pocket, with enemy air assets dominating the area (ukr_leaks_eng/30282).
Drone groups from the Rubicon unit penetrated deep into the Donetsk and Luhansk regions, striking logistical targets behind front lines (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian forces reported 27 UAV strike impacts across eastern locations, indicating persistent Russian aerial pressure (kpszsu/62230).
Multiple Shahed swarms entered the eastern sector from Kursk, Bryansk and Belgorod, targeting settlements near Donetsk (kpszsu/62230).
Ukrainian air defenses downed numerous drones over the east, but some penetrated, causing minor infrastructure damage (kpszsu/62230).
Russian reconnaissance drones equipped with thermal imaging increased night‑time activity, complicating Ukrainian concealment (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian artillery units reported engaging drone formations near the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, preventing deeper incursions (kpszsu/62202).
Shahed drones launched from the north (Orel, Kursk) entered Ukrainian airspace, with Ukrainian defenses claiming 180 UAVs downed overall (kpszsu/62230).
Ukrainian radar detected two controlled UAVs operating between Synylnykovym and Slavhorod, indicating possible reconnaissance activity (eRadarrua/92611).
Drone groups from Belgorod approached the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, threatening northern supply routes (kpszsu/62202).
Ukrainian forces reported a new group of strike drones in Sumy heading toward Shostka, increasing pressure on northern logistics (kpszsu/62215).
Air raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 18:55, reflecting heightened northern aerial threat (eRadarrua/92618).
Russian Rubicon drones with dual cameras operated over northern territories, enhancing strike precision (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian anti‑air units successfully intercepted multiple UAVs over the north, limiting casualties (kpszsu/62230).
Approximately 20 Shahed drones entered Odesa from the Black Sea, striking residential districts and causing civilian alarm (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Kharkiv‑region drones also targeted southern Odesa, with additional groups approaching Bolhrad and passing Vilkove westward (kpszsu/62216).
Ukrainian reports from Izmail district indicate a UAV hit a warehouse roof, igniting a fire but causing no casualties (UkraineNow/81303).
Drone swarms over Kharkiv from the south added pressure to the southern approaches of the city (kpszsu/62260).
Ukrainian air defenses downed numerous drones over the south, though some penetrated, damaging civilian infrastructure (UkraineNow/81303).
Russian missile capabilities demonstrated by the Priluk strike suggest potential for further long‑range attacks on southern targets (UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian units reported increased artillery engagements against drone formations near the Odesa coastline (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Ukrainian air defenses claimed 180 enemy UAVs shot down, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across all fronts (kpszsu/62230).
A MiG‑31K launched from Savasiysk carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, marking a new high‑speed strike capability for Russia (mon1tor_ua/66835).
Russian Rubicon drones equipped with dual day/thermal cameras operated deep behind Ukrainian lines, increasing the threat to ground forces (ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian forces reported 27 successful UAV strikes by Russia on 15 locations, indicating continued offensive aerial operations (kpszsu/62230).
Multiple drone groups from various Russian regions (Belgorod, Kursk, Orel) penetrated Ukrainian airspace, prompting widespread alerts (kpszsu/62230, kpszsu/62202).
Ukrainian anti‑air units engaged and destroyed drone formations over Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia, limiting damage (kpszsu/62214, kpszsu/62204, kpszsu/62260).
Air raid alerts over Kyiv and other major cities reflect heightened aerial threat levels (eRadarrua/92618).

key_developments: A Russian ballistic missile struck central Priluk, killing two civilians and injuring 17, damaging a factory, mall and supermarket (Source: @UkraineNow/81317).
Ukrainian air defenses reported downing 180 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas models, across the north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62230).
A MiG‑31K fighter launched from Savasiysk airfield carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, indicating escalation of long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66835).
Approximately 20 Shahed drones entered Odesa from the Black Sea, striking residential areas and prompting civilian alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66830).
Russian Rubicon unit deployed dual‑camera drones deep behind Ukrainian lines, increasing strikes on rear areas and engaging Ukrainian UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30272).
Ukrainian troops north of Pokrovsk are trapped in a Russian‑controlled kill zone, with enemy pilots dominating all approaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30282).

Multiple drone groups penetrated Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia regions, targeting Chuhuiv, Pavlograd, Kamyanske and other strategic points (Source: @kpszsu/62214, @kpszsu/62204, @kpszsu/62260, @kpszsu/62202).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are expanding their long‑range strike portfolio, demonstrated by the Priluk ballistic missile and the Kinzhal launch, while intensifying UAV assaults across all fronts. Ukrainian air defenses remain effective, downing large numbers of drones, yet Russian Rubicon UAVs with advanced sensors are penetrating deep rear areas, threatening logistics and command nodes. The encirclement of Ukrainian units near Pokrovsk further constrains Ukrainian maneuverability in the east.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply air defense systems, reflected in the high UAV interception rates, but diplomatic efforts show little progress as Russia maintains a hardline stance. China’s engagement with Russia, highlighted by Putin's visit, may bolster Russian strategic options, while NATO statements on Kaliningrad underscore rising regional tensions. Ukraine’s internal political focus remains on maintaining morale and addressing civilian hardships.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates a surge in coordinated drone swarms from multiple Russian regions, suggesting a shift toward saturation tactics. The deployment of dual‑camera Rubicon drones points to improved Russian ISR capabilities, necessitating revised Ukrainian concealment measures. The Kinzhal launch from Savasiysk may signal testing of hypersonic strike routes targeting critical infrastructure beyond the front lines.
